summary: First end-to-end test of Kindred. Backend: 21/21 pytest cases pass (auth, household, statement upload via Claude Sonnet 4.5, budget, chat, family-thread, audit-log, participant). Frontend: full caregiver journey works — login, dashboard with 3 streams + lifetime cap + anomaly card, statements list, AI chat (LLM response references caregiver name, classification, quarter & burn), family thread, audit log, statement upload page, participant view (huge text + giant 'Call' & 'Concern' buttons), logout, fresh signup → onboarding → dashboard. No JS page errors. All data-testid selectors are present and reliable.

minor.issue: Returns 403 (FastAPI default for missing HTTPBearer) — acceptable, but 401 would be more conventional
Static sample appointment (Sarah, 10:00 AM, Personal care) — acknowledged in PRD as MOCKED; calendar agent deferred to v1.1

issues: No nav link in sidebar for the Statement Upload page — user can only reach it via the 'Upload a statement' CTA on the dashboard. Minor UX nit; not a blocker.

action_items: Optional: Convert /api/auth/me missing-token response from 403 → 401 for convention.
Optional (v1.1): Replace MOCKED static appointment in /api/participant/today with a real calendar feed.
Optional UX: Add a sidebar nav entry for 'Upload statement' so users can jump there from any page.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 545 lines — borderline. Consider splitting into routers (auth/household/statements/budget/chat/family/audit/participant) before it grows further.
MongoDB ObjectId is correctly excluded everywhere via {'_id': 0} projections — good.
Lazy-load of EMERGENT_LLM_KEY in agents.py works around dotenv ordering — keep this pattern.
Pydantic EmailStr correctly rejects .test/.invalid TLDs — make sure UI validation messages mention this so users don't get stuck on 422s.
Statement upload accepts .csv/.pdf/.txt; consider validating MIME type explicitly and rejecting other types with a clear 415 instead of attempting decode.
/api/participant/today appointment is static — flag clearly in UI ('sample data') so participants don't trust it as live.
test_credentials: cathy@example.com / testpass123 (seeded). New signup uses test+<timestamp>@example.com.
seed_data_creation: Each backend run creates a fresh test+<timestamp>@example.com user, a 'Mum' Classification 4 household, plus uploads /tmp/test_stmt.csv-equivalent CSV against cathy's household. UI test creates uitest+<timestamp>@example.com with Classification 3 household. No teardown — accumulates test users; consider periodic cleanup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sample CSV at /tmp/test_stmt.csv triggers rate-spike anomaly on the 26 Apr cleaning row ($95 vs $75.50 median) — confirmed working. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY + Claude Sonnet 4.5 is live; statement upload + chat take 5–20s. Use real-looking email domains (.com, not .test) because Pydantic email-validator rejects reserved TLDs. Nav testids are derived from labels with spaces→hyphens: nav-dashboard / nav-statements / nav-ask-kindred / nav-family-thread / nav-audit-log. Statement upload has no nav entry — reach via dashboard-upload-cta button.
rca of the issue: No bugs to RCA — first build is clean.
